title: Host deadline service — one unified one-shot, bytecode timer facilities, and late-fire walls
purpose: The deadline pillar governed by `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:74-89` at `c9bb64a`. Receive-after currently leaves a relative `milliseconds` record with the cooperative scheduler and the wrapper arms every record as its own `HostTimer`/`setTimeout` (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:24-33`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:727-741`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:688-773`). Native `Deliver` timers instead carry absolute `web_time::Instant` expiries in the shared wheel; true idle exposes the wheel minimum, but the wrapper only serializes that value and never arms it (`crates/beamr/src/timer.rs:47-65`; `crates/beamr/src/timer.rs:233-237`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:614-625`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1117-1135`). INFERRED from those bytes: a VM whose only future work is a native deadline can remain parked until an unrelated event, while N receive-after timers create N host one-shots. The immediate and post-drain settled-idle checks also consult only `timer_handles`, so a native-deadline-parked process can currently resolve `await_exit` as idle before its future `Deliver` is host-armed (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:553-583`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:597-632`). WPORT-3 replaces those split reaches with one wrapper-owned deadline service, injects the already-existing timer facility into cooperative bytecode execution, and makes late-but-complete delivery countable without polling or promptness claims. Binding law: “A one-shot callback for a known deadline is event delivery; a recurring callback that checks the timer wheel is a design error and a tear condition” (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:85-87`).
task:
Implement the domain-owner DECIDED service shape: wrapper-owned unified deadline state plus one opaque host handle. The wrapper owns (a) a receive map from `timer_id` to `{pid, absolute_deadline}` and (b) the native earliest learned from the completed drain result; it computes their minimum and owns the only active deadline callback. Receive records are relative durations today (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:24-33`), so stamp every newly drained receive schedule against one wrapper `web_time::Instant::now()` sample in the same monotonic clock domain used by the native wheel (`crates/beamr/Cargo.toml:34-39`). Receive schedule/cancel mutations already converge through the queues drained by wrapper sync, including `send_message` and every completed drain (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:212-220`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:421-430`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:130-139`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:521-539`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:688-713`); native mutations occur inside cooperative slices and true idle already reports the resulting wheel minimum (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:373-386`; `crates/beamr/src/native/context/mod.rs:1043-1129`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:614-625`). Therefore add no scheduler deadline-event vocabulary and no scheduler-level unified query: expose the absolute native deadline already carried by `WasmRunState::Idle` through an additive accessor if the wrapper needs it, then reconcile at the existing sync/drain convergence seams. Candidate A (wrapper-owned map) is the domain-owner position; candidates B/C remain tear decision ground only, and candidate D is rejected because retaining per-receive callbacks cannot satisfy the arc's one-callback scope (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:79-87`).

REVIEWER-TRACED GROUND, carried verbatim as a design constraint: “the arbiter's ignored_callbacks supersede is callback-count-based, not identity-based — in the fairness-macrotask/manual-drain/external-microtask interleave the live microtask can be swallowed and the stale macrotask drains; invariant pending == ignored + (state==Queued) holds; exactly one callback drains. THEREFORE: re-arm logic must key off drain-completion state (the settled result), NEVER off which callback identity ran. Re-arm at perform_drain/finish_drain-adjacent seams, not inside callback identities.” The current implementation increments a scalar on manual supersession, lets whichever queued callback arrives first consume it, and only then consults the shared arbiter state (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:478-518`); drain completion alone decides idle versus the next fairness turn (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:521-550`). A wrapper-local arm generation/token may reject a cleared callback that races with `clearTimeout`, but it MUST NOT decide post-drain re-arming or couple the deadline service to the identity of the arbiter callback that happened to drain.

SETTLED IDLE inherits WPORT-2 Ruling 3 exactly: “WPORT-3 inherits the same definition when native deadlines start being armed: a pending native deadline means not settled.” (`docs/design/beamr/briefs/WPORT-2.json:12`). Extend the predicate to TRUE IDLE and no pending deadline of either class; both immediate `await_exit` and post-drain waiter resolution must use that one predicate after deadline reconciliation, closing the current native-deadline gap (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:553-583`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:597-632`).

OPEN QUESTIONS FOR THE TEAR, named rather than smuggled: (1) service placement — the domain-owner wrapper-owned shape versus scheduler-event vocabulary; record candidates A-C as decision ground and reject D against the frozen one-callback scope; (2) receive-timer absolute-deadline bookkeeping — wrapper stamps at sync (the domain-owner call) versus scheduler-side absolute records, with clock-domain correctness deciding; (3) PROBE-THROTTLE prototype scope/timing — the brief carries the browser+Worker artifact spec, while the tear decides when it runs. Any overturn of the decided service shape, any need for new scheduler event vocabulary, or any inability to preserve a single monotonic clock is STOP-and-report.

requirements.title: Replace per-receive one-shots with one wrapper-owned deadline service across receive-after and native Deliver
requirements.spec:
Create one per-VM unified deadline service in the wasm wrapper. Its receive map is keyed by `timer_id` and stores `pid` plus a wrapper-stamped absolute `web_time::Instant`; its native component is the absolute earliest native deadline from the completed `WasmRunState::Idle`; its active arm is one `{armed_deadline, opaque_handle, local_arm_token}` record, where `local_arm_token` exists only to reject a cleared callback that still runs. The opaque value returned by `setTimeout` must be passed back verbatim to `clearTimeout`; the current receive path already stores a `JsValue` handle and passes it through verbatim (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:715-773`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1153-1173`). Add `clearTimeout` to the constructor-probed `HostPrimitives` alongside the already-probed `queueMicrotask` and `setTimeout`, rather than retaining the current per-call, silently-ignored lookup (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:342-351`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1166-1173`). Add a direct wrapper dependency on the repository's existing `web-time` version if needed so receive stamping and native absolute values share the same monotonic clock (`crates/beamr/Cargo.toml:34-39`; the current wrapper dependency list is `crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:13-29`).

Retire `BTreeMap<u64, HostTimer>` and every per-timer receive closure. At each wrapper sync, drain cancellations first, remove their receive-map entries, sample `now` once, and insert every schedule at `now + milliseconds`; then reconcile. At each completed drain, synchronize receive records, update the native component from the settled result, and reconcile at a `perform_drain`/`finish_drain`-adjacent seam before resolving idle waiters. Reconciliation computes `min(receive_earliest, native_earliest)`: unchanged means no host call; an earlier/different minimum clears the old opaque handle and arms one new one-shot for a saturating non-negative delay; no minimum clears the arm. A later non-earliest insertion is a no-op. Cancelling the current earliest clears or moves the arm; cancelling a non-earliest entry is a no-op at the host. On FAIRNESS YIELD, no native deadline value is available (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:67-82`): do not infer one or settle idle; retain/reconcile the known receive component, and rely on the already-queued fairness continuation plus the next drain-start native tick until eventual TRUE IDLE reports the wheel minimum (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:507-512`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:614-625`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:542-550`).

The one shared deadline callback must admit only its still-current local arm token, consume that active arm before delivery, sample one deterministic `now`, call `tick_native_timers_at(now)` so every due native `Deliver` is removed and delivered, collect every receive-map entry with deadline `<= now`, remove those entries, and call id-based `scheduler.timer_fired(pid, timer_id)` for the full due set. The core fire is already stale-safe for a missing/mismatched receive id (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:222-238`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_tests.rs:201-220`), and native ticking already removes all due entries before mailbox delivery (`crates/beamr/src/timer.rs:201-219`; `crates/beamr/src/timer.rs:259-292`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:468-495`). After the complete due set is delivered, consume the Rust ready edge once, request the WPORT-2 arbiter once for this admitted deadline event (never once per expiry), and defer all next-arm decisions until that drain settles. A stale cleared arm token performs no delivery, no arbiter request, and no state/counter mutation.

Replace both current settled-idle checks with one helper evaluated only after reconciliation: TRUE IDLE and unified pending-deadline state empty. A receive or native deadline — armed as the one known-deadline callback — means not settled. `await_exit` for a process parked behind a native `Deliver` must remain pending, then resolve `exited` after the deadline event delivers and the arbiter drains it. Preserve late delivery: neither receive fire nor native tick rejects a timer because `now` is later than its requested instant (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:222-238`; `crates/beamr/src/timer.rs:201-219`). NO-POLLING binds every branch: “A one-shot callback for a known deadline is event delivery; a recurring callback that checks the timer wheel is a design error and a tear condition” (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:85-87`).
acceptance: At every observable point, the unified deadline service owns `queued_now` 0 or 1 across receive-after and native `Deliver`; the old per-timer `HostTimer` map/closures are gone, and a mixed burst never creates a second deadline callback

An earlier insertion clears the previous opaque handle and arms exactly once at the earlier absolute deadline; a later insertion leaves the handle, arm count, and armed-deadline snapshot unchanged; cancelling the earliest moves to the next minimum or clears if none

One admitted fire at an arbitrarily late deterministic `now` ticks the native wheel, fires every due receive id, consumes the active edge, requests one arbiter turn for the complete due set, and exposes/arms the next deadline only after that drain settles

A cleared/replaced callback that still executes is rejected by local arm token and cannot clear the live arm, duplicate delivery, request a turn, or drive re-arm logic; arbiter callback identity is never consulted

`await_exit_waits_for_armed_native_deliver_timer` (proposed name; the tear may rename it) parks a process behind a future native `Deliver`, proves the Promise does not resolve `idle`, then proves deadline delivery drives the target to `exited` with its expected result

`idle_vm_schedules_zero_recurring_callbacks` is strengthened rather than replaced: TRUE IDLE with one future deadline has deadline `queued_now = 1` and a stable armed-deadline value, while an intervening host macrotask causes zero new arms/executions and zero arbiter churn; the permitted one-shot is counted, not ignored
The existing receive settled-idle wall `await_exit_waits_for_armed_receive_timer` remains green under the unified group (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1515-1555`)

requirements.title: Inject the native timer wheel into cooperative bytecode NativeServices and prove BIF parity
requirements.spec:
Set `timers: Some(Arc::clone(&self.native_timers))` in `WasmScheduler::native_services`. At `c9bb64a`, that constructor sets only `atom_table` and `wasm_async_nif_facility`, so `NativeServices::default()` leaves the timer option absent (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:719-725`; `crates/beamr/src/interpreter/mod.rs:46-65`). Cooperative native actors already receive the same wheel (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:373-386`). Make no BIF, `ProcessContext`, registration, payload, reference, or cancellation semantic changes: Gate 1 already registers `send_after/3`, `start_timer/3`, and `cancel_timer/1` (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:42-56`); the BIFs and context already schedule/cancel `TimerKind::Deliver` entries when a facility exists (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:254-304`; `crates/beamr/src/native/context/mod.rs:1043-1129`).

The current missing-service refusal must disappear specifically on cooperative bytecode execution: absent `self.timers` makes schedule/reserve/cancel return `None` (`crates/beamr/src/native/context/mod.rs:1043-1129`), which maps `send_after` and `start_timer` to `badarg` and `cancel_timer` to `false` (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:254-304`). Acceptance must execute real bytecode through `run_with_native_services`, not call the BIF functions directly. Prove `send_after/3` returns a reference and later delivers the original message; `start_timer/3` returns a reference and later delivers `{timeout, Ref, Msg}`; `cancel_timer/1` returns remaining milliseconds for a pending reference and `false` on a second cancel. The existing direct-context round-trip provides the semantic baseline but not cooperative scheduler coverage (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:943-989`). Because these BIFs mutate the same native wheel, a bytecode-scheduled future timer must become the native component of R1's unified minimum and arm the one host callback when the drain settles idle.
acceptance: `cooperative_bytecode_timer_bifs_round_trip_and_arm_unified_deadline` (proposed name; the tear may rename it) runs bytecode `send_after/3`, `start_timer/3`, and `cancel_timer/1` under the cooperative scheduler; it proves reference returns, original-message delivery, exact `{timeout, Ref, Msg}` delivery, remaining-ms then `false`, and no `badarg`/missing-service refusal

The same test snapshots the unified deadline service after bytecode schedules a future native timer and proves exactly one host arm at that timer's deadline; adding the second/later BIF timer does not create a second callback
No source change is made to `crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs`, `crates/beamr/src/native/context/mod.rs`, timer payload construction, BIF registration, or threaded service construction

requirements.title: Pin late-fire, stale-fire, and cancellation-race semantics in Node while carrying PROBE-THROTTLE honestly
requirements.spec:
Add Node-pinnable service-level walls using a test-only deterministic fire seam that invokes the same shared callback logic at a supplied `web_time::Instant`. A fire well after multiple receive and native deadlines must deliver the complete due set exactly once, not merely one bucket/class; native `tick_at` already expires every entry whose `expires_at <= now`, removes it before return, and the cooperative scheduler delivers each `Deliver` to the mailbox (`crates/beamr/src/timer.rs:201-219`; `crates/beamr/src/timer.rs:259-292`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:468-495`). Preserve receive delayed-delivery correctness: matching ids wake at the timeout continuation without a wall-clock comparison, while a message-won/cancelled id makes the later callback stale and harmless (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:222-238`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:421-430`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_tests.rs:201-220`). Preserve native cancellation races: cancel-before-fire prevents expiry; fire removes the entry, so cancel-after-fire returns `false` and cannot retract the already-produced message (`crates/beamr/src/timer.rs:190-199`; `crates/beamr/src/timer.rs:259-292`; `crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:288-304`). Pin cancel-versus-host-callback and clear/re-arm races through R1's stale arm-token wall.

Carry, but do not promote, the platform prototype as `docs/design/beamr/probes/WPORT-3-PROBE-THROTTLE.md` (proposed location; the tear may rename/relocate it). The artifact specifies two manual runs against the real generated wasm bundle: (a) background a browser tab with an armed receive and native deadline; (b) run the same workload in a Worker and background/throttle its owner. Capture requested deadline, observed callback time, delivery identities/count, next arm, runtime/browser/OS versions, and whether each context fires late-but-completely. This is explicitly NON-CI and UNVERIFIED-ON-PLATFORM. T2's bound is quoted, not converted into repository truth: “Browsers throttle timers in backgrounded tabs — `setTimeout` clamping at minutes scale exists in the wild, and Worker contexts differ again — so a one-shot armed at the earliest deadline delivers LATE under throttling, by design of the platform” (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:81-87`). The acceptance law is also exact: “No acceptance test in this brief may assert timing precision that flakes under throttling, and no liveness claim rides deadline promptness” (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:81-87`).

State the proof boundary in the handoff: Node proves deterministic late due-set completeness, id/token stale-fire safety, cancellation outcomes, one arbiter request, and next-deadline selection. Node does not prove background-tab clamp amount, Worker scheduling differences, or real-platform promptness; those remain PROBE-THROTTLE territory. The existing native actor test already proves no early delivery at +5 seconds and successful deliberately-late delivery at +15 seconds (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native_tests.rs:336-420`); extend the wall to the unified service rather than replacing that core test.
acceptance: The FIRE-DELIVERS-ALL wall uses a deterministic late `now` and a mixed due set; every due receive timeout and native `Deliver` is observed exactly once, one arbiter turn is requested, a not-yet-due entry remains pending, and the next deadline is armed after settle

The CANCEL-EARLIEST/RE-ARM-EARLIER walls invoke a captured cleared callback after cancellation/replacement and prove stale-fire safety: the live arm/deadline, delivery count, transition counters, and arbiter count are unchanged
The BYTECODE-BIF wall proves cancel-before-fire suppresses delivery, and cancel-after-fire returns `false` without retracting the already-delivered message
Delayed receive delivery remains id-based and late-safe: a late matching id takes the timeout continuation; a message-won id is stale and cannot wake twice
No Node assertion contains a maximum callback latency, near-deadline tolerance, browser/Worker availability claim, or liveness dependency on prompt firing

The handoff records `PROBE-THROTTLE: NOT RUN — UNVERIFIED-ON-PLATFORM` and the artifact location unless the tear separately orders the prototype; any later observations remain evidence attached to that artifact, not CI acceptance

requirements.title: Expose unified deadline transitions and make seven exact acceptance walls permanent in the Node gate
requirements.spec:
Replace/extend the receive-only instrumentation group with a separately named unified deadline group. The current group has only `requests`, `queued_now`, `executions`, and `cancellations`, and its receive `queued_now` increments once per per-timer arm (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:377-389`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:643-667`). Preserve those host-call totals, but make unified `queued_now` a true 0/1 gauge and add transition observables that classify the frozen cases: `rearms_earlier`, `cancel_moves_or_clears`, `later_noops`, and `next_arms` (or tear-approved equivalent names), plus the exact `armed_deadline: Option<web_time::Instant>` in the test snapshot. `requests` counts actual host arms, `cancellations` counts actual `clearTimeout` calls, `executions` counts admitted active-arm fires, and the reason counters classify why those totals changed; stale callbacks change none. Expose this only through the existing non-exported `#[cfg(all(test, target_arch = "wasm32"))]` snapshot pattern (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1175-1211`), with no production diagnostic ABI.

Add exactly seven new in-crate wasm-bindgen acceptance names, proposed below; the tear may rename them, but any rename must update this brief's mapping and the workflow inventory in the same commit. (1) CARDINALITY — `unified_deadline_keeps_one_host_callback_for_mixed_timer_burst`; (2) RE-ARM-EARLIER — `earlier_deadline_cancels_and_rearms_the_unified_callback`; (3) CANCEL-EARLIEST — `cancelling_earliest_deadline_moves_or_clears_the_unified_callback`; (4) LATER-NO-OP — `later_deadline_does_not_rearm_the_unified_callback`; (5) FIRE-DELIVERS-ALL — `late_fire_delivers_all_due_timer_classes_queues_one_turn_and_arms_next`; (6) SETTLED-IDLE-NATIVE — `await_exit_waits_for_armed_native_deliver_timer`; (7) BYTECODE-BIF — `cooperative_bytecode_timer_bifs_round_trip_and_arm_unified_deadline`. Fold R3's stale callback/cancellation interleaves into the relevant seven pins rather than adding timing-precision tests.

Keep all current eleven exact names green (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:41-64`). The three conversion tests remain byte-untouched (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/convert.rs:390-501`). Strengthen `idle_vm_schedules_zero_recurring_callbacks` so it asserts a pending unified one-shot is visibly armed (`queued_now = 1` and stable `armed_deadline`) while arbiter and deadline request/execution totals do not recur across a host macrotask; do not manufacture an idle pass by ignoring/subtracting the permitted callback. Extend `arbiter_installation_rejects_missing_host_primitive` in-place to include `clearTimeout` refusal while retaining its exact name. Migrate `await_exit_waits_for_armed_receive_timer` to the unified snapshot without weakening it. The current workflow enumerates all eleven exact names and requires the exact eleven-pass summary, failing missing names and wrong/zero discovery (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:41-72`); update the array with the seven new fully qualified names and require exactly `18 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 filtered out` in the same commit.

Perform, record, and revert three fail-first mutations: (a) permit a second concurrent unified deadline arm and show CARDINALITY fails; (b) skip the clear/re-arm on an earlier insertion and show RE-ARM-EARLIER fails; (c) feed the workflow assertion one stale/missing expected name and show the job fails despite otherwise passing test output. NO-POLLING remains the counted wall: “A one-shot callback for a known deadline is event delivery; a recurring callback that checks the timer wheel is a design error and a tear condition” (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:85-87`).
acceptance: CARDINALITY: `unified_deadline_keeps_one_host_callback_for_mixed_timer_burst` schedules a mixed receive/native burst and proves unified `queued_now` never exceeds 1, with one opaque live handle

RE-ARM-EARLIER: `earlier_deadline_cancels_and_rearms_the_unified_callback` starts armed at T2, adds T1<T2, and observes exactly one clear, one new arm at T1, one `rearms_earlier`, and no duplicate queued callback

CANCEL-EARLIEST: `cancelling_earliest_deadline_moves_or_clears_the_unified_callback` cancels T1, proves the armed snapshot moves to T2 or becomes `None`, and proves a racing stale T1 callback cannot disturb the result
LATER-NO-OP: `later_deadline_does_not_rearm_the_unified_callback` starts at T1, adds T3>T1, and observes no new request/cancellation, one `later_noops`, the same armed deadline, and `queued_now = 1`

FIRE-DELIVERS-ALL: `late_fire_delivers_all_due_timer_classes_queues_one_turn_and_arms_next` fires late over a mixed due set, proves complete exactly-once delivery, one arbiter request, one admitted execution, and the next known deadline armed after settle
SETTLED-IDLE-NATIVE: `await_exit_waits_for_armed_native_deliver_timer` keeps completion pending while the native one-shot is armed and resolves `exited` only after fire and drain
BYTECODE-BIF: `cooperative_bytecode_timer_bifs_round_trip_and_arm_unified_deadline` proves cooperative bytecode send/start/cancel semantics and the shared host arm

Existing eleven names remain green; `idle_vm_schedules_zero_recurring_callbacks` counts the armed one-shot rather than waving it away; `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml` names all eighteen tests and asserts the exact eighteen-pass summary while zero discovery still fails
All seven new pins and every changed existing pin pass ten consecutive isolated runs; fail-first evidence for second arm, missing earlier re-arm, and stale CI inventory is captured and reverted

boundaries: NO WPORT-2 scheduling redesign: the drain contract, arbiter state machine, and coalescing invariant are stable surface — extend at the named sync/perform_drain/finish_drain seams only. The `ignored_callbacks` mechanism is not reworked; identity-independence constrains deadline re-arm design (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:442-550`).

NO TimerWheel semantic changes (additive accessors/queries only if required); no BIF or ProcessContext semantic changes. Facility injection makes existing timer semantics reachable (`crates/beamr/src/timer.rs:113-237`; `crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:254-304`; `crates/beamr/src/native/context/mod.rs:1043-1129`).
NO threads-surface changes; the wasm closure remains `cooperative,json` (`crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:13-21`).
NO browser/Worker platform claim in CI acceptance. PROBE-THROTTLE is a NON-CI tear prototype and remains UNVERIFIED-ON-PLATFORM (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:81-87`).

NO promptness assertions; liveness never rides deadline promptness. “No acceptance test in this brief may assert timing precision that flakes under throttling, and no liveness claim rides deadline promptness” (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:81-87`).

Laws stated where they bind: NO-POLLING means a known-deadline one-shot is event delivery and a recurring wheel check is a tear condition (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:19-23`; `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:85-87`); believed state is not citable state, so every current beamr-state assertion is pinned to file:line at `c9bb64a`, never to a research artifact.
verification: Run the house bar on the final head: `cargo fmt --check`; touch every modified Rust file and run fresh `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`; run fresh `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`; run `cargo test --workspace --all-features --lib`; run `cargo test --workspace --all-features --test '*'`; and run `npm test --prefix examples/edge-worker`. Run both locked wasm checks retained at `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:17-20`: `cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p beamr --no-default-features --features cooperative,json --locked` and `cargo check --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked`. Then run the pinned Node leg: `CARGO_TARGET_WASM32_UNKNOWN_UNKNOWN_RUNNER=wasm-bindgen-test-runner cargo test --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked -- --nocapture`; require the updated workflow assertion to find every exact existing/new name and the exact `18 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 filtered out` summary, with missing names, stale names, wrong counts, and `no tests to run!` still failing. Run all seven new pins and every changed existing pin in isolation ten consecutive times. Record final `git rev-parse HEAD`, `rustc --version --verbose`, `cargo --version`, `wasm-bindgen-test-runner --version`, `node --version`, wasm target, UTC timestamps, exact commands, one-time `queueMicrotask`/`setTimeout`/`clearTimeout` probe results, unified counter and armed-deadline snapshots for every frozen transition, settled-idle trigger point, exact eighteen-name inventory, and reverted fail-first outputs. Record `PROBE-THROTTLE: NOT RUN — UNVERIFIED-ON-PLATFORM` with `docs/design/beamr/probes/WPORT-3-PROBE-THROTTLE.md` unless the tear separately orders the prototype; no browser/Worker observation is required or accepted as CI proof.
